claim: Provider configuration maintains isolation between Claude, Codex, and custom providers in demand evidence mode, with boundary enforcement preventing project mutations and tool restriction flags properly managed per provider.

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentProvider function maps provider aliases (anthropic->claude, openai->codex, shell/local->custom) and returns null for auto/empty, preventing provider ambiguity.
evidence.why: Provider normalization is the first line of defense against cross-provider leakage.

evidence.summary: selectedProvider function normalizes provider selection and defaults to claude when provider is empty or normalization fails.
evidence.why: Ensures predictable provider defaults during evidence dispatch.

evidence.summary: Codex provider uses isolated configuration path: codexModel from ai.codex_model or modelValue(ai), codex_sandbox and codex_approval flags, with output-last-message file capture.
evidence.why: Codex has separate command-line flags and model handling from Claude.

evidence.summary: executionConfig clears ai.model when switching to codex if it contains claude, preventing model inheritance across providers.
Custom provider uses sh -c with rendered command supporting ${model} interpolation, with no explicit file access restrictions in provider-adapter.
evidence.why: Custom providers have different security model than managed providers.
Claude has comprehensive tool and session management flags not used by other providers.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot creates SHA-256 digests for all project files, walking directory tree and using git ls-files when available, with exclusion for .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache.
evidence.why: Foundation for detecting project mutations during evidence gathering.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after file digests to detect added, modified, or deleted files, returning sorted change list.
evidence.why: Mutation detection logic that flags boundary violations.

evidence.summary: execution_policy defines forbidden project_mutation and allowed_write_roots restricted to output directory, with agent_instruction boundary_controlled.
evidence.why: Formal boundary policy declaration for evidence agents.

evidence.summary: executionConfig for claude provider in evidence mode disables session persistence (claude_no_session_persistence: true) and clears tool/settings flags (ai.settings='', claude_tools='', claude_allowed_tools='').
evidence.why: Evidence mode imposes strict isolation on Claude sessions.

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime captures boundary snapshots before/after agent execution, diffing them to detect violations and adding BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers when changes detected.
evidence.why: Runtime boundary enforcement with actual mutation detection.

evidence.summary: Test verifies codex dispatch does not inherit claude default model (claude regex fails on runOptions.config.ai.model), codex_sandbox and codex_approval remain undefined for flexibility.
evidence.why: Concrete test proving codex configuration isolation from claude defaults.

evidence.summary: Test verifies claude dispatch clears settings, claude_tools, claude_allowed_tools to empty strings, sets claude_no_session_persistence to true, and passes max_budget_usd through.
evidence.why: Test proving Claude tool restriction clearing in evidence mode.

evidence.summary: Tests verify full tool profile requires explicit allowFullAgentTools authorization, and research profile is normalized while keeping tools available.
evidence.why: Tool profile authorization guardrails are tested.

evidence.summary: Boundary mutation tests verify project file writes are blocked even when agent results pass, including git-backed detection of untracked and gitignored files.
evidence.why: Comprehensive boundary enforcement coverage including git edge cases.

evidence.summary: Non-git boundary test verifies file walk fallback detects modifications while ignoring artifacts in allowed write roots.
evidence.why: Boundary enforcement works without git repository.

evidence.summary: buildAgentAdapterCapabilities returns provider-specific capability contracts including sandbox modes, file_write/shell_exec permissions, and budget enforceability per provider.
evidence.why: Provider capabilities are formally separated and documented.

assumptions: Custom provider commands are assumed to be trusted by the user when explicitly configured in ai.custom_command.
Provider-specific defaults (like codex_sandbox workspace-write) are assumed safe for evidence mode workloads.
Git ls-files availability is assumed for optimal boundary detection, with file walk fallback when git is unavailable.
risks: A provider-specific bug in model field clearing (line 448) could cause cross-provider model leakage.
Custom provider with arbitrary sh -c commands could bypass intended boundary restrictions if configured maliciously.
Boundary detection might miss file mutations in very large repositories with slow git operations or excluded directories.

result.notes: Provider configuration maintains isolation through separate code paths, explicit model clearing, and boundary enforcement. Test coverage validates cross-provider separation and mutation detection. Risks are mitigated through design and testing.
